description: African American prison gang, street gang, and political organization founded in 1966 by George Jackson, George 'Big Jake' Lewis, and W.L. Nolen at San Quentin State Prison in California. It is a Marxist-Leninist revolutionary group promoting black power and prisoner dignity, involved in drug trafficking, burglary, and homicide. The BGF remains active with estimated 100-300 members and thousands of associates.
